description: Use a Ralph-style 4-lane tmux swarm to keep Stage 1 current-truth consolidation grounded in source truth, bounded feature/condition/shadow surfaces, explicit policy/risk/settlement owners, continuous docs sync, and receipt-backed follow-on runtime hardening on the path to a governed Solana-first backtesting and paper-trading platform.

userStories.title: Bootstrap the repo-local orchestration pack
userStories.description: As a repo operator, I want a tracked .ai pack with lane guides, a current repo index, a shared dropbox shape, and a story ledger so future multi-agent loops stop inventing structure or rediscovering existing surfaces.
acceptanceCriteria: The repo contains a tracked .ai control surface with lane guides, templates, and a current repo map
The repo contains a canonical story ledger and append-only progress surface
The structure makes writer-integrator the only owner of story advancement and accepted docs truth
The current repo map explicitly names the implemented source, feature, condition, and shadow surfaces plus the still-missing policy, risk, and settlement owners
userStories.notes: Completed in the orchestration bootstrap slice on 2026-04-15.
userStories.whyNow: Keep the control plane, docs truth, and acceptance loop monotonic before widening capability.
userStories.mustNotWiden: Do not widen beyond Stage 1 current-truth consolidation until orchestration, docs truth, and owner contracts are clean.

userStories.title: Add tmux and Ralph runner wrappers for the swarm
userStories.description: As a repo operator, I want repo-local wrappers for starting, inspecting, capturing, and stopping a four-lane tmux session, plus a local Ralph runner, so the loop is repeatable and not just a chat convention.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/agents contains wrappers for starting, inspecting, sending to, capturing, and stopping the swarm
The wrappers default to four lanes with the fixed research, builder, architecture, and writer-integrator roles
The repo contains a local scripts/ralph/ralph.sh runner and matching prompt assets for Codex-based iteration
The builder lane can be launched with Codex Spark and the architecture lane can be launched with JetBrains-guided review prompts
userStories.notes: Completed in the orchestration bootstrap slice on 2026-04-15.
userStories.whyNow: Keep the control plane, docs truth, and acceptance loop monotonic before widening capability.
userStories.mustNotWiden: Do not widen beyond Stage 1 current-truth consolidation until orchestration, docs truth, and owner contracts are clean.

userStories.title: Document and validate the swarm contract
userStories.description: As a repo operator, I want one current runbook and one test surface for the orchestration pack so future loops can trust the session shape and repo-local state rules.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/runbooks contains a runbook for the Ralph/tmux swarm
docs/README and project inventory mention the orchestration surface
The repo has a contract test that checks the presence and minimal shape of the orchestration files
The docs continue to state that policy, risk, and settlement are still the remaining runtime owners
userStories.notes: Completed in the orchestration bootstrap slice on 2026-04-15.
userStories.whyNow: Keep the control plane, docs truth, and acceptance loop monotonic before widening capability.
userStories.mustNotWiden: Do not widen beyond Stage 1 current-truth consolidation until orchestration, docs truth, and owner contracts are clean.

userStories.title: Add continuous completion supervision and receipt-backed acceptance
userStories.description: As a repo operator, I want the four-lane swarm to keep cycling until no eligible stories remain and the final architect plus writer audit is clean, with acceptance receipts and recovery flow making semantic progress explicit instead of inferred.
acceptanceCriteria: prd.json carries explicit story state fields that make eligibility and progression machine-runnable
The swarm writes structured acceptance receipts and uses them to update story state and progress truth
The persistent supervisor keeps looping until no eligible stories remain, then runs a final architect plus writer completion audit
Docs, prompts, and contract tests describe the continuous completion semantics clearly
userStories.notes: Completed in the continuous-completion supervision slice on 2026-04-16.
userStories.whyNow: Keep the control plane, docs truth, and acceptance loop monotonic before widening capability.
userStories.mustNotWiden: Do not widen beyond Stage 1 current-truth consolidation until orchestration, docs truth, and owner contracts are clean.

userStories.title: Open the first explicit policy decision-trace owner
userStories.description: As a repo operator, I want policy to consume bounded condition truth explicitly so strategy eligibility and no-trade outcomes stop living as implication or future intent.
acceptanceCriteria: The repo has a first explicit policy contract that consumes bounded condition truth such as global_regime_v1
Policy outputs include explicit no-trade states and traceable eligibility decisions
The contract leaves advisory and shadow modeling outside runtime authority
Docs and tests describe the policy boundary clearly
userStories.whyNow: Land explicit strategy-eligibility policy truth instead of implied downstream behavior.
userStories.mustNotWiden: Do not widen beyond Stage 1 current-truth consolidation until orchestration, docs truth, and owner contracts are clean.

userStories.title: Open the first explicit hard risk gate
userStories.description: As a repo operator, I want risk to become the final veto on top of explicit policy output so bounded condition and policy surfaces still cannot imply paper action on their own.
acceptanceCriteria: The repo has a first hard risk gate surface with explicit veto outcomes
The risk gate consumes policy output instead of condition output directly
Stale-data, anomaly, and unresolved-state paths default safely to no-trade or halted paper behavior
Tests prove that risk remains the final gate
userStories.whyNow: Keep hard veto logic explicit before any runtime or strategy widening.
userStories.mustNotWiden: Do not widen beyond Stage 1 current-truth consolidation until orchestration, docs truth, and owner contracts are clean.

userStories.title: Open paper session and settlement ownership
userStories.description: As a repo operator, I want settlement to own paper session state, paper fills, and feedback receipts so the engine can close the loop honestly without pretending shadow evidence is equivalent to execution truth.
acceptanceCriteria: The repo has an explicit paper session and settlement owner surface
Paper fills and paper session receipts are stored in a truthful runtime home
Feedback and reporting are tied to settlement truth rather than only experiment artifacts
The docs clearly distinguish settlement truth from shadow evidence
userStories.whyNow: Make paper settlement and reporting truthful before later backtest or execution expansion.
userStories.mustNotWiden: Do not widen beyond Stage 1 current-truth consolidation until orchestration, docs truth, and owner contracts are clean.

userStories.title: Add the terminal completion contract and finder-governed follow-on flow
userStories.description: As a repo operator, I want backlog exhaustion, audit-found follow-ons, and truly terminal completion to become explicit top-level swarm states so the four-lane loop can stay honest when seeded stories are done but governance still has receipt-backed next work to decide.
acceptanceCriteria: prd.json carries explicit top-level swarm completion fields such as swarmState and completionAuditState, and the active follow-on orchestration story makes backlog exhaustion distinct from terminal completion.
The supervisor, health, and status surfaces distinguish active work, backlog exhaustion, audit-followons-present, and terminal-complete states without treating stale mailbox noise as canonical truth.
Architecture-finder and research-finder exist as mode switches inside the existing lanes, emit advisory audit artifacts, and never mutate backlog truth directly.
Writer-integrator remains the only promotion authority for finder outputs, and docs/tests describe the terminal-completion plus audit-followon contract clearly.
userStories.notes:
Promoted from the 2026-04-16 architecture completion audit to make backlog exhaustion, audit-known follow-ons, and terminal completion governed states instead of implicit swarm behavior.

userStories.title: Open explicit continuous capture runtime ownership
userStories.description: As a repo operator, I want continuous capture to become an explicit runtime owner with lane freshness states, cadence expectations, and operator-visible health so downstream feature, policy, risk, and settlement decisions depend on governed ingest continuity instead of ad hoc manual capture runs.
acceptanceCriteria: The repo defines explicit runtime expectation classes plus freshness and completeness rules for active Jupiter, Helius, Coinbase, and FRED capture lanes without widening readiness-only surfaces into runtime authority.

An operator-visible status or doctor surface resolves each governed capture lane to explicit freshness states and downstream eligibility using repo-owned truth such as ingest_run, source_health_event, and capture_cursor where it is actually authoritative.
Downstream runtime consumers fail closed on stale or degraded required capture lanes under the explicit source owner instead of relying on implicit manual capture assumptions.
Docs, blocker tracking, and validation artifacts describe continuous capture ownership as an accepted runtime owner rather than an active missing blocker.
userStories.whyNow: Keep source freshness and provider truth clean before widening downstream dependence.
userStories.mustNotWiden: Do not widen beyond Stage 1 current-truth consolidation until orchestration, docs truth, and owner contracts are clean.

userStories.title: Scope-lock completion-audit finder runs and make state sync monotonic
userStories.description: As a repo operator, I want completion-audit finder work to carry explicit scope and drive one canonical swarm state so terminal-complete claims cannot coexist with pending finder work, blank active-story fallbacks, or mismatched audit packets.
acceptanceCriteria: With completion_audit finder scope pending, architecture and research finder runs emit only completion_audit__* outputs and out-of-scope finder artifacts are ignored or superseded.
Runtime launch, active, and completion markers carry finder scope so status and health surfaces can distinguish scoped finder work from story work.
prd.json, lane_health.*, and completion_audit_writer.json cannot present terminal_complete or clean while completion-audit finder work is pending or being decided.
A targeted orchestration validation proves one sync path is sufficient to make finder_state, prd.json, and lane_health.* agree on completion-audit state transitions.
userStories.notes:
Stage 1 current-truth consolidation is active: the durable descent guide now lives in docs/issues/governed_product_descent_capability_ladder.md, the missing capability layers are decomposed in docs/gaps/, and writer-integrator must keep the entire docs tree aligned with accepted code, prd.json, and progress.txt as ORCH-006 closes.

userStories.whyNow: Keep the control plane, docs truth, and acceptance loop monotonic before widening capability.
userStories.mustNotWiden: Do not widen beyond Stage 1 current-truth consolidation until orchestration, docs truth, and owner contracts are clean.

userStories.title: Bind research-loop comparison to realized paper outcomes
userStories.description: As a repo operator, I want research_loop to consume settlement-owned paper receipts so shadow experiments are evaluated against realized paper outcomes instead of only upstream traces and experiment artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: research_loop consumes settlement-owned paper receipts as comparison inputs without promoting shadow outputs into runtime authority.
Experiment comparison can evaluate bounded shadow receipts against realized paper outcomes from paper_session, paper_fill, paper_position, and paper_session_report.
Feedback-governance docs and tests make clear that realized comparison informs research and promotion review without self-promoting models or strategies into runtime eligibility.
Docs, blocker tracking, and the repo map no longer describe realized-feedback comparison as a missing blocker once the accepted comparison path exists.
userStories.whyNow: Bind realized paper outcomes back into research comparison without promoting research into runtime authority.
userStories.mustNotWiden: Do not widen beyond Stage 1 current-truth consolidation until orchestration, docs truth, and owner contracts are clean.

userStories.title: Open the first runtime-owned execution-intent surface
userStories.description: As a repo operator, I want a bounded execution-intent owner between risk and settlement so allowed risk verdicts can become explicit spot-only mint, size, and entry-exit intent without inventing hidden strategy authority or implying live execution behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: The repo has an explicit execution-intent contract downstream of risk_global_regime_gate_v1 and upstream of PaperSettlement instead of treating a market-wide risk verdict as full intent by implication.
The execution-intent owner persists or selects quote-backed spot-only mint, size, and entry-exit intent and fails closed on ambiguous, unsupported, or stale intent inputs.
Settlement consumes the explicit execution-intent surface rather than requiring manual explicit ids at every call site or inferring hidden strategy selection from risk alone.
Docs, blocker tracking, and tests describe execution intent as a bounded paper-only owner that remains separate from policy eligibility, promotion, and live trading authority.
userStories.notes: Deferred until SOURCE-001 and RESEARCH-001 land because execution intent should follow the source-owner and realized-feedback hardening work, not jump ahead of them.
userStories.whyNow: Close the remaining execution-intent owner gap between risk and settlement before widening.
userStories.mustNotWiden: Do not widen beyond Stage 1 current-truth consolidation until orchestration, docs truth, and owner contracts are clean.

userStories.title: Define the backtesting truth model
userStories.description: As a repo operator, I want governed backtesting truth for sessions, fills, slippage, fees, latency, and realized PnL so future strategy work compares against a truthful paper/backtest model instead of shadow-only evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: The repo has an explicit backtesting truth contract that distinguishes tradeable instruments, bucket boundaries, fill assumptions, and realized PnL.
Spot, perps, and futures assumptions are differentiated without widening runtime authority early.
The docs packet explains how backtests, paper sessions, and future instrument widening relate.
Backtesting truth is staged after the current blocker backlog instead of bypassing source ownership or execution intent.
userStories.notes: Deferred until current blocker stories and execution-intent hardening are farther along; this story family defines the next governed product descent rather than the current active slice.
userStories.whyNow: Define truthful backtest semantics before judging broader strategy quality.
userStories.mustNotWiden: Do not widen into later-stage strategy or instrument expansion until prerequisite stages are accepted.

userStories.title: Define the market regime and labeling program
userStories.description: As a repo operator, I want canonical regime and direction labels with explicit horizons, confidence handling, and evaluation metrics so future model comparisons are governed by one math truth surface.
acceptanceCriteria: The repo defines canonical `up`, `down`, `flat`, invalid, and low-confidence label classes with explicit horizon and threshold rules.
The repo defines a bounded regime taxonomy with explicit unknown and stale-input behavior.
Evaluation metrics are explicit enough to compare strategy and forecast ideas honestly.
The labeling program is documented as a future governed product surface rather than immediate runtime authority.
userStories.notes: Deferred until the current blocker backlog is farther along; this is a north-star story family for the future strategy program.
userStories.whyNow: Define one canonical regime and label program before expanding strategy families.
userStories.mustNotWiden: Do not widen into later-stage strategy or instrument expansion until prerequisite stages are accepted.

userStories.title: Define governed strategy comparison and promotion candidates
userStories.description: As a repo operator, I want a governed strategy-evaluation layer that compares advisory research outputs against backtesting and paper truth without letting promising models silently become runtime authority.
acceptanceCriteria: The repo defines how candidate strategies are compared across regimes and instrument stages.
Research outputs such as Chronos-2, Monte Carlo, Fibonacci, autoresearch, and ANN-style modeling are explicitly advisory until promoted.
Promotion candidates have explicit contracts, receipts, and rollback expectations before they can influence runtime owners.
The strategy-comparison story family stays staged behind current blocker work and future backtesting truth.
userStories.notes: Deferred until current blockers plus backtesting and labeling truth are ready; strategy work should not jump ahead of the governing truth surfaces.
userStories.whyNow: Add strategy-family comparison only after backtest and label truth are governed.
userStories.mustNotWiden: Do not widen into later-stage strategy or instrument expansion until prerequisite stages are accepted.
